name: Duck Miramonte
recipeInstructions:
-------------
Wine Sauce:
-----------
6 ounces butter
1 1/2 cups (duck jellied stock or demi-glaze)
5 shallots, chopped
1 teaspoon cracked peppercorns
2 medium tomatoes, seeded and chopped
Optional: 5 cepes mushrooms
2 cups Cabernet Sauvignon wine
Bone Marrow:
------------
7 ounces bone marrow, chopped
Salt
Spinach:
--------
1 bunch spinach
1 nutmeg
2 ounces butter
Pepper
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Bone the duck and reserve any parts for stock. Trim breasts, salt, and pepper them. Add trimmings to other bones for stock.
3. In a sauté pan, melt butter over medium heat. Place breasts skin side down, melting fat between skin and meat (1-2 minutes). Increase heat to high and brown lightly. Transfer pan with breasts to oven and cook 8 minutes.
4. For wine sauce, melt butter in copper pan with shallots and peppercorns (1-2 minutes). Add cepes, Cabernet Sauvignon, reduce slightly. Stir in stock or demi-glace and tomatoes if desired, cooking 20-30 minutes over medium heat. Add butter if desired, whip, and set aside.
5. For bone marrow, bring salted water to boil in a pan. Cook marrow for about 5 minutes. Set aside.
6. Blanch spinach in hot, salted water, then press dry. In sauté pan, melt butter until brown (beurre rosette). Add spinach, nutmeg, salt, and pepper, cooking 20 seconds (spinach should start to wilt).
7. Serve wine sauce as a pool on plate, add bone marrow to sauce. Trim duck breasts skin side down, slice thin. Arrange slices in sauce on plate, garnish with watercress and spinach.
